generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors internal box environment temperature for real-time thermal regulation. Part of the incubator's closed-loop control system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary internal temperature sensor providing redundant measurement for internal box environment. Used for cross-validation in calibration algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors ambient external temperature to account for environmental disturbances in thermal modeling and control decisions."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureData
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Cyber-physical interface aggregating sensor inputs (temperature, environmental) and forwarding to Digital Twin framework. Acts as embedded controller for the incubator system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ControllerAggregatedData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance InternalTemperatureData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
    base:desc "Real-time temperature readings from primary internal sensor transmitted to Digital Twin for model calibration and monitoring."
]

instance InternalTemperatureData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
    base:desc "Real-time temperature readings from secondary internal sensor transmitted to Digital Twin for redundancy and cross-validation."
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Real-time ambient temperature readings transmitted to Digital Twin for environmental disturbance modeling."
]

instance ControllerAggregatedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom RaspberryPiController
    base:desc "Aggregated sensor data package including internal/external temperatures forwarded from Raspberry Pi to Digital Twin framework."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified differential equation-based thermal model with 2 free parameters for predicting internal temperature dynamics. Used as foundational abstraction for incubator behavior."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, PredictiveSimulationEnabler
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-fidelity differential equation-based thermal model with 4 free parameters accounting for heatbed thermal inertia. Demonstrates superior accuracy in modeling complex thermal dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, PredictiveSimulationEnabler
]

instance GeometricIncubatorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "CAD-like geometric representation of incubator box dimensions and structural properties without dynamic behavior. Used for spatial context in simulations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance RealTimeTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuously sampled temperature data from all sensors merged into unified time-series dataset for model validation and control decisions."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateEstimationEnabler, CalibrationDataValidationEnabler
]

instance HistoricalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Archived temperature data used for long-term trend analysis, model parameter optimization, and validation against experimental results."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelVersionControlEnabler
]

instance ControllerCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Optimized control parameters (e.g., heatbed power, fan speed) generated by Digital Twin for physical system actuation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PhysicalActuationInterface
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Implements parameter estimation algorithms (e.g., least squares, Bayesian inference) to reconcile discrepancies between simulated and real-world temperature data. Updates model parameters continuously."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService, PredictiveSimulationService
]

instance PredictiveSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Executes differential equation solvers to simulate thermal behavior under current or hypothetical conditions, accounting for sensor noise and environmental variability."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveSimulationService, WhatIfScenarioTestingService
]

instance StateEstimationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Uses sensor fusion techniques (e.g., Kalman filters) to mitigate noise and provide accurate temperature state estimates for control decisions."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, SafetyValidationService
]

instance CalibrationDataValidationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Validates sensor data quality and model predictions against predefined thresholds, triggering alerts for data anomalies or model drift."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataQualityAssuranceService
]

instance OptimizationControllerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Implements optimization algorithms to dynamically adjust control parameters for energy efficiency, robustness, and minimal temperature overshoot/undershoot."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlOptimizationService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Generates interactive visualizations (e.g., time-series plots, 3D thermal maps) using geometric and thermal models for engineering communication and decision support."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance ModelVersionControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Manages iterative refinement of models and algorithms based on new data or environmental changes, maintaining version history for model evolution."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelEvolutionService
]

instance PhysicalActuationInterface : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Translates Digital Twin control commands into physical actuator signals (e.g., PWM for heatbed, fan speed) via Raspberry Pi hardware interface."
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicalActuationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuously acquires and processes sensor data to provide current state estimation and anomaly detection for the incubator system."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureStateInsight, AnomalyDetectionAction
]

instance PredictiveSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes dynamic thermal models to predict system behavior under current or hypothetical operating conditions, accounting for environmental disturbances."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalBehaviorForecastInsight
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Iteratively updates thermal models by comparing simulated outputs with real-time sensor data to ensure high-fidelity representation of physical system."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight, CalibrationUpdateAction
]

instance SafetyValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Deploys monitoring algorithms to assess whether the physical system operates within safe and optimal temperature bounds, triggering interventions when violations occur."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyComplianceInsight, CorrectiveActionAction
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamically adjusts control parameters (e.g., heating power, fan speed) to achieve energy efficiency, robustness, and minimal temperature deviations."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalControlParametersInsight, ParameterAdjustmentAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates advanced visualizations of temperature trajectories, model predictions, and system performance metrics for engineering analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalVisualizationInsight
]

instance DataQualityAssuranceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Validates sensor data quality and model predictions against experimental results to ensure data integrity and model reliability."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataIntegrityInsight
]

instance ModelEvolutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Manages versioned evolution of models and algorithms through iterative refinement based on new data or environmental changes."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelVersionInsight, ModelUpdateAction
]

instance PhysicalActuationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes control commands generated by Digital Twin to adjust physical system actuators (e.g., heatbed, fan) for temperature regulation."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuationExecutionInsight
]

instance WhatIfScenarioTestingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulates impact of sensor failures or environmental changes on system performance to support robust decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides ScenarioImpactInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current and historical temperature measurements from all sensor locations, providing real-time state of the incubator environment."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically identifies temperature deviations beyond predefined thresholds, triggering alert notifications."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ThermalBehaviorForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted temperature trajectories under current or hypothetical operating conditions, accounting for environmental disturbances and control inputs."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative metrics comparing simulated vs. real-world temperature data to validate model fidelity."
]

instance CalibrationUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically updates model parameters based on calibration algorithms to maintain high-fidelity representation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SafetyComplianceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of whether current operating conditions comply with safety temperature bounds and optimality criteria."
]

instance CorrectiveActionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjusts physical system configuration (e.g., heating power) when safety violations are detected, either automatically or via human intervention."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance OptimalControlParametersInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommended control parameters (e.g., heatbed power, fan speed) optimized for energy efficiency and temperature regulation performance."
]

instance ParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically applies optimized control parameters to physical actuators for temperature regulation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ThermalVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D thermal maps and time-series plots of temperature distributions within the incubator and its environment."
]

instance DataIntegrityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results comparing sensor data quality and model predictions against experimental benchmarks."
]

instance ModelVersionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Version history and performance metrics of all thermal models, including calibration dates and accuracy improvements."
]

instance ActuationExecutionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Confirmation of executed control commands and their impact on physical system behavior."
]

instance ScenarioImpactInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted system behavior under hypothetical conditions (e.g., sensor failure, environmental changes) to support risk assessment."
]

instance ModelUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically updates model versions based on new data or environmental changes to maintain system relevance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary heating element responsible for generating thermal energy to maintain target temperature within the incubator container."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Air circulation device ensuring uniform temperature distribution inside the incubator box by preventing thermal stratification."
]

// C1: Environment/Component
instance IncubatorPhysicalEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical space containing the incubator system with controlled thermal conditions."
    base:contains InsulatedContainerComponent, HeatbedActuator, FanActuator,
                    InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance InsulatedContainerComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Styrofoam box providing thermal insulation to minimize heat loss from the incubator environment."
]

instance RaspberryPiHardware : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Embedded controller platform aggregating sensor inputs and executing control algorithms for the incubator system."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT exerts control over the Physical Twin (PT) via **command signals** generated by algorithms (e.g., supervisory controllers, optimizers) to regulate the incubator's temperature. These commands are executed by the **Raspberry Pi**, which interfaces with the **heatbed** and **fan** to adjust thermal output. The **control message format** is not explicitly specified, but the system implies **closed-loop feedback** where the DT continuously evaluates sensor data (e.g., internal/external temperature) to refine control actions. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not detailed, though the use of **calibration algorithms** suggests a form of **parameter validation** to ensure model accuracy before command execution. **Feedback systems** are inherent to the DT architecture, as monitors (e.g., fault detectors) detect deviations from optimal performance and trigger corrective actions. **Conflict resolution** is not explicitly addressed, but the hierarchical structure (DT → Raspberry Pi → PT) implies prioritization of **safety-critical commands** (e.g., overheating prevention) over non-critical adjustments. **Command execution confirmation** is inferred through the **monitoring loop**, where the DT verifies the PT's response (e.g., temperature stabilization) post-command."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator case study operates with a **real-time synchronization mechanism** between the Physical Twin (PT) and its Digital Twin (DT) via sensor data acquisition. The system collects **temperature data** from two internal sensors and one external sensor at a **high temporal granularity**, though the exact frequency is not specified in the documents. The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update its models (e.g., differential equation-based thermal models) with the latest sensor data, ensuring the DT's predictions align with the PT's current state. The latency requirements are implied to be **low** to enable real-time monitoring and control adjustments, such as fault detection or supervisory controller interventions when deviations (e.g., unsafe or suboptimal temperature conditions) are detected. The DT’s temporal granularity is dictated by the **sampling rate of the sensors** and the **computational overhead of the calibration and simulation processes**, with a trade-off between accuracy and simulation speed (e.g., more detailed models with four free parameters are slower but more accurate)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation for the incubator follows a **monolithic, service-oriented architecture** centered around three core components: **data acquisition** (sensor inputs), **model-based simulation** (thermal dynamics), and **algorithm-driven control** (calibration, monitoring, and optimization). The orchestration is structured as follows:
  1. **Data Flow**: Sensor data (temperature) from the PT is streamed to the DT for real-time calibration of the thermal models.
  2. **Model Integration**: The DT employs **two thermal models** (one with 2 free parameters, another with 4) to represent the PT’s behavior at varying levels of abstraction. The four-parameter model is preferred for its accuracy in capturing thermal inertia.
  3. **Algorithm Execution**: Calibration algorithms adjust model parameters to minimize discrepancies between simulated and observed temperatures. Monitors then evaluate the PT’s performance (e.g., safety, optimality) and trigger corrective actions via supervisory controllers or human intervention.
  The system lacks explicit **resource management** (e.g., cloud-edge partitioning) or **distributed coordination protocols**, as it operates as a standalone CPS-based DT. The architecture is **tightly coupled** to the PT’s hardware (Raspberry Pi controller, heatbed, sensors) and software stack, with no mention of external orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the provided context includes the following phases: **1. Modeling Phase**: Abstract representations of the Physical Twin (PT) are created at varying levels of abstraction, such as CAD models (e.g., geometric and structural details of a car chassis) or dynamic models (e.g., differential equations for analyzing forces and movement). These models may range from static (e.g., CAD) to dynamic (e.g., physics-based simulations) and are tailored to specific analysis goals (e.g., understanding behavior, optimization, or fault detection). **2. Integration Phase**: Models are integrated into the DT framework alongside **data** (collected via sensors from the PT over time) and **algorithms** (e.g., calibration, fault detection, supervisory controllers, or state estimators). This phase involves updating models with real-time sensor data to ensure alignment with the PT's current state. **3. Operational Phase**: The DT operates in parallel with the PT, using **calibration algorithms** to refine model parameters based on sensor inputs. Monitors within the DT continuously assess system performance (e.g., safety and optimality) and trigger corrective actions (e.g., adjustments to the PT or algorithmic interventions) when deviations are detected. **4. Evolution Phase**: The DT is designed to adapt to changes in the PT or its environment, leveraging **reconfigurability** and **robustness** to maintain accuracy. This phase may involve iterative updates to models, algorithms, or data pipelines to accommodate new operational conditions or system modifications. **Lifecycle Governance**: The documents imply a governance approach centered on **continuous integration of models and data**, **real-time validation** (via monitors), and **human-in-the-loop decision-making** for critical adjustments. The lifecycle emphasizes preserving models post-deployment to enable ongoing value extraction from the PT."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s **model fidelity** and **validity** are governed by:
  - **Fidelity Requirements**: Models must balance **abstraction** (e.g., CAD for static geometry) and **detail** (e.g., differential equations for dynamics). The incubator study demonstrated that **higher-fidelity models** (4-parameter vs. 2-parameter) improved accuracy in predicting **thermal inertia**, a critical physical property.
  - **Validation and Verification (V&V) Methods**: **Experimental validation** (e.g., comparing simulated vs. real incubator temperatures) and **parameter tuning** (e.g., calibrating free parameters using sensor data) ensure model alignment with PT behavior. The DT framework employs **calibration algorithms** to iteratively refine model parameters based on real-time data.
  - **Uncertainty Sources**: Quantified through **model discrepancy** (e.g., prediction errors in the 2-parameter vs. 4-parameter incubator models) and **sensor noise**. Uncertainty is mitigated via **higher-fidelity models** and **data-driven corrections**.
  - **Error Detection and Correction**: **Monitors** within the DT detect deviations (e.g., unsafe operating conditions) and trigger **corrective actions** (e.g., adjusting actuators or alerting operators). The incubator’s **controller** likely implements **feedback loops** to maintain temperature within specified bounds.
  - **Quality Assurance (QA) Processes**: Continuous **model-data comparison** (e.g., validating thermal predictions against sensor readings) and **algorithm-driven optimizations** (e.g., supervisory controllers) ensure sustained DT accuracy. The open-source incubator hardware/software provides a **verifiable platform** for testing and refining these processes."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT leverages a **cyber-physical system (CPS) architecture** where the **Raspberry Pi** serves as the primary communication node between the PT and DT. The **network topology** is centralized, with the Pi aggregating sensor data and relaying control commands. **Communication protocols** are not specified, but the system implies **low-latency, real-time data exchange** due to the dynamic thermal regulation requirements. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are critical for maintaining synchronization between the PT and DT, though exact metrics (e.g., <100ms latency) are not provided. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not explicitly described, but the use of **multiple sensors** (three temperature sensors) suggests fault tolerance for critical data streams. **Security protocols** are not mentioned, though the DT’s reliance on **calibration algorithms** and **closed-loop control** implies a need for **data integrity** and **authenticated command execution** to prevent adversarial interference."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT infrastructure is **edge-deployed**, with the **Raspberry Pi** hosting the core computational workloads (e.g., model calibration, monitoring, control algorithms). **Computational requirements** are modest, as the system relies on **simplified thermal models** (e.g., differential equation-based predictors with 2–4 free parameters) rather than high-fidelity simulations. **Storage requirements** are minimal, focusing on **real-time sensor data** and **model parameter updates**. **Scalability mechanisms** are not addressed, but the modular design (e.g., replaceable models) allows for incremental complexity. The **deployment model** is **on-premise/edge-centric**, with no mention of cloud integration. **Availability/reliability assurance** is implied through the **closed-loop monitoring** and **calibration feedback**, though no explicit redundancy (e.g., backup Pi nodes) or failover protocols are described. The system prioritizes **real-time responsiveness** over high availability, given its safety-critical thermal regulation role."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system for the incubator case study incorporates **safety-critical system considerations** through its **monitoring and calibration mechanisms**, as depicted in Figure 1. The DT employs **monitors** that continuously check whether the **Cyber-Physical System (CPS)** and its environment are performing **safely and optimally** by leveraging real-time data from sensors (e.g., temperature sensors inside and outside the insulated container). **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implied through the DT’s ability to detect violations (e.g., temperature deviations) and trigger **human or algorithmic interventions** to adjust the physical twin (PT) via the **Raspberry Pi controller**. While the documents do not explicitly detail **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, authentication systems) or **access control mechanisms**, the DT’s reliance on **sensor data integration** and **calibration algorithms** suggests a need for secure data transmission and validation to prevent adversarial tampering or spoofing. **Risk assessment approaches** are indirectly referenced through the DT’s role in **predicting system behavior** and **optimizing performance**, though no specific methodologies (e.g., ISO 26262, NIST frameworks) are mentioned. The system’s **reconfigurability** (e.g., adapting to changing environmental conditions) implies robustness, but no explicit **threat mitigation strategies** (e.g., intrusion detection, anomaly detection) are described."
]
